---
title: "Policy and Value Functions in a HANK Model"
subtitle: "Computational Economics Final Project"
author:
  - name: Lorenzo Castro, Paolo Diotallevi
    affiliations: Collegio Carlo Alberto
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    fig-align: center
    smooth-scroll: true
    page-layout: article
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

::: {.callout-note}
# Computational Economics Final Project

This report was prepared for the Computational Economics final project. It documents the policy functions, value functions, and diagnostic figures generated by the Julia code in this repository.
:::

# Paper and Replication Package

This project studies policy and value functions in a HANK-style model. The main goal is to generate and visualize model-based objects such as consumption, labor, savings, value functions, and flow utility.

The code is organized as follows:

```text
src/HANKPolicies.jl                 Core Julia module
scripts/hank-figures.jl             Generates the main report figures
scripts/policy_value_plots.jl       Generates supplementary diagnostics
figures/figure1.png                 Main report Figure 1
figures/figure1.pdf                 PDF version of Figure 1
figures/figure2.png                 Main report Figure 2
figures/figure2.pdf                 PDF version of Figure 2
output/*.png                        Supplementary generated diagnostics
```

There is no separate raw-data folder in the project. The objects reported below are generated directly from the model. The final report figures are stored in `figures/`, while additional diagnostic plots are stored in `output/`.

# High-Level Description

The project focuses on the numerical analysis of policy and value functions in a heterogeneous-agent New Keynesian model. The computational task is to solve the model, generate the relevant policy functions, and visualize how the model outcomes change across different state variables.

In particular, the report studies consumption, labor, savings, value functions, and flow utility. These objects help describe how agents behave in the model and how the model solution changes across asset levels and cash-on-hand values.

# Main Results

The two figures in this section are the main report outputs. They are loaded directly from the `figures/` directory. The corresponding `.pdf` files are also available in the same folder for publication or LaTeX workflows.

![Main model-generated report figure.](figures/figure1.png){#fig-figure1 width="72%"}

![Second main model-generated report figure.](figures/figure2.png){#fig-figure2 width="72%"}

# Supplementary Diagnostics

The figures below are generated by `scripts/policy_value_plots.jl` and saved in `output/`. They are included as supplementary diagnostics to better understand the model solution.

## Policy Functions

The first group of figures shows how the policy functions vary with assets and cash-on-hand. These plots are useful for checking whether the model solution behaves in a reasonable way across the relevant state space.

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![Consumption policy by assets.](output/policy_consumption_vs_assets.png){#fig-cons-assets}

![Consumption policy by cash-on-hand.](output/policy_consumption_vs_cash_on_hand.png){#fig-cons-coh}

![Labor policy by assets.](output/policy_labor_vs_assets.png){#fig-labor-assets}

![Labor policy by cash-on-hand.](output/policy_labor_vs_cash_on_hand.png){#fig-labor-coh}
:::

The next figure reports the savings policy as a function of assets.

![Savings policy by assets.](output/policy_savings_vs_assets.png){#fig-savings-assets width="70%"}

## Value and Utility

The final diagnostic figures report the value function and flow utility. These figures provide additional checks on the shape of the model solution.

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![Value function by assets.](output/value_function_vs_assets.png){#fig-value-assets}

![Flow utility by assets.](output/flow_utility_vs_assets.png){#fig-flow-assets}
:::

# Reproducibility

The Julia environment is defined by `Project.toml` and `Manifest.toml`.

To reproduce the outputs from the repository root, run:

```bash
julia --project=. -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.instantiate()'
julia --project=. scripts/hank-figures.jl
julia --project=. scripts/policy_value_plots.jl
```

The expected output locations are:

```text
figures/      Main report figures
output/       Supplementary policy, value, and utility plots
```

To render the report, run:

```bash
quarto render report.qmd
```
